calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Irie Pushia (Petitioner).

The Court approved the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-08-2011 Continued To Nov-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Vacate Order Confirming Sale, Order Resale Of The Property, And For Damages To The Estate Against The Purchaser
Off Calendar Sep-21-2011 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Continued To Sep-21-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Receipt For Blocked Account
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Continued To Sep-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Vacate Order Confirming Sale, Order Resale Of The Property, And For Damages To The Estate Against The Purchaser
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Continued To Sep-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Receipt For Blocked Account
Off Calendar Aug-03-2011 Continued To Aug-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for Irie Pushia.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that Mortgage Broker told her that there was one procedural appraisal condition, that the Lender is going to drive by the property and take a look at it to make sure it has the value of the amount they are lending and that she was told by Mr. Lee (Real Estate Broker) that they are willing to give the Estate $50.00 a day beginning July 27, 2011. The matter was continued to August 24, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Lofdahl is to express to the Broker the Court's concerns and frustrations.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Tauscher.

Petition To Vacate Order Confirming Sale, Order Resale Of The Property, And For Damages To The Estate Against The Purchaser
Off Calendar Aug-03-2011 Continued To Aug-24-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Vacate Order Confirming Sale, Order Resale Of The Property, And For Damages To The Estate Against The Purchaser
Off Calendar Jul-20-2011 Continued To Aug-03-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for Irie Pushia (Petitioner).

Ms. Lofdahl requested a two week continuance stating that the Escrow Officer was expecting to receive the closing papers on Monday but that he had not received them. The matter was continued to August 3, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Oct-27-2010 Continued To Jan-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for Irie Pushia. Robert Lee, Real Estate Agent also present.

Ms. Lofdahl requested a continuance stating that the Buyers' financing fell through, that they were working on other financing, that the Buyer was prepared to increase the down payment to $16,000.00 and that the Heir wanted to put the property back on the market. In addition, she stated that there were no others Buyers, that she did not think putting it back on the market was the right thing to do, spoke of other alternatives, and requested that the Court allow payment of the home owner's insurance, the property taxes and the fee for removal of some of the personal property be paid from the funds in the blocked account. The matter was continued to January 5, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status. The Court also advised Ms. Lofdahl to file an ex parte application with regard to use of funds from the blocked account for said payments.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1242 Revere Street, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Aug-27-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Irie Pushia. Donald Lee from Embassy Realty also present. Donald Sanders (Grandson of Decedent) also present.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that Mr. Lee represented the Buyer and the Seller, that he would waive service, that the Buyer qualified for a FHA loan which requires 5% down, that they put down $9,000.00, and that Mr. Lee would make up the balance in order to assist with the sale going through. In addition, Ms. Lofdahl asked the Court to waive the 10% down payment requirement, stated that the commission would be split between the Buyer and the Seller, and that part of the commission would be used for the down payment. Mr. Sanders stated that he was objecting to the sale and that the property was being sold for a low amount. Mr. Lee explained the efforts made with regard to listing the property and the open houses, and stated that there were no other interested Buyers. There were no other Parties in Court bidding on the property. The sale was confirmed at $330,000.00. The Court granted the petition (subject to written waivers from the Buyers and Ms. Lofdahl submitting a completed order). The Court dispensed with a verifed supplement to the petition. The proceeds from the sale of the property are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status for filing of the receipt for blocked account was set for September 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for Irie Pushia.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that there was not a trmendous amount of cash in the estate, that her Client did not have access to any bank records, that there was a joint account held with the Conservatee and that $1,000.00 were found. In addition, Ms. Lofdahl stated that an eviction was in process, that the property was going to be sold as soon as possible and that she anticipated filing the receipt for the blocked account by Wednesday. The matter was continued to February 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Counsel to file receipts for any other sums of funds going into the estate.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Irie Pushia.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that she was seeking limited authority because her Client did not meet the bonding requirements and stated that funds would be placed into a blocked account. The petition was granted (signing the order for the same). Bond is not required and the funds are to be placed into a blocked account.
